context: The remote branch had been reverted to 34fc6f72 (see remote-revert-to-34fc6f72.json), dropping the deterministic card grid. The board owner supplied a validated-working mural-journal-sync-layout.js using row-index placement and asked to combine it with the previously-correct tag handling.
change: Adopted the supplied mural-journal-sync-layout.js. Placement is deterministic via placementForRow(layout, rowIndex): x = COLUMN_X[category] (Perceptions 120, Procedures 456, Decisions 792, Introspections 1128), width 288, height 168, y = COLUMN_START_Y 264 + rowIndex * ROW_PITCH 192. rowIndex is the entry's position within its category, so placement does not depend on detecting the previous card and the stacking regression is fixed.
Retained Snowberry project-tag preservation/truncation, D1 project-name resolution, and blank-template marker-stripping in the layout module.
Retained Mint user-tag creation, tag preservation on PATCH, and restyle of pre-existing user tags in mural-journal-sync-safe-tags.js (unchanged across the revert).
Reconciled the remote revert by merging while keeping the row-index layout and updated tests; kept the remote 2026/06/15 revert trace.
constraints: The Mural public sticky-note create/update API accepts only text, x, y, backgroundColor — no width, height, or font size. Cards cannot be resized and body text cannot be scaled to fit via the API; long entries overflow the fixed 168px box.
residualRisks: No live Mural mutation was performed from this session; coverage is via mocked Mural API responses.
Row-index placement assumes the journal Mural matches the fixed template grid (true for template duplicates).
